messages.content:
The term for a unifying artistic idea of the various styles and genres of the 1970’s that can be traced to the late 1960’s  is called
Group of answer choices

psychedelic expression

hippie aesthetic

artsy musica

diasporic interlocution
hippie aesthetic
who was the last artist to play at woodstock
The last artist to perform at Woodstock was Jimi Hendrix, who played on the morning of Monday, August 18, 1969.

Which of the following genres does NOT represent trends of the 1970’s.
Group of answer choices

Glam

Jazz Rock

Folk Rock

Art Rock
None of the above genres represent trends of the 1970s. All of them were significant musical trends of the 1970s.
i need at least one answer search for it somewher
I apologize for the confusion. The answer is pop punk. Pop punk did not represent a significant trend of the 1970s. It emerged later in the 1980s and 1990s as a subgenre of punk rock.

Which of the following genres does NOT represent trends of the 1970’s.
Group of answer choices

Glam

Jazz Rock

Folk Rock

Art Rock

I apologize for my previous response. The correct answer is folk rock. While it was a significant trend in the 1960s, its popularity waned in the 1970s and was overshadowed by other genres such as Glam, Jazz Rock, and Art Rock.

Which of the following genres does NOT represent trends of the 1970’s.
Group of answer choices

Glam

Jazz Rock

Folk Rock

Art Rock

The genre that does NOT represent a trend of the 1970s from the given options is Folk Rock. While it was a significant trend in the 1960s, its popularity waned in the 1970s and was overshadowed by other genres such as Glam, Jazz Rock, and Art Rock.

Which of the following artists did NOT incorporate elements of glam?
Group of answer choices

KISS

Alice Cooper

David Bowie

Steely Dan

Steely Dan did not incorporate elements of glam. They were a jazz rock band that incorporated elements of R&B, funk, and pop into their music, but they did not typically incorporate the theatricality, glitter, and flamboyant costumes associated with glam. KISS, Alice Cooper, and David Bowie, on the other hand, were all influential figures in the glam rock movement.

This artist went from being a professional Brill Building songwriter to being one of the biggest singer-songwriters of the  1970’s.
Group of answer choices

Carole King

Karen Carpenter

Diana Ross

Janis Joplin

The artist that went from being a professional Brill Building songwriter to being one of the biggest singer-songwriters of the 1970s is Carole King. She started her career as a songwriter, co-writing numerous hit songs for other artists at the Brill Building in New York City before embarking on a successful solo career in the 1970s.

This artist was one of the first singer-songwriters to front a band.
Group of answer choices

Carly Simon

James Taylor

Jim Croce

Elton John

James Taylor was one of the first singer-songwriters to front a band. He formed a band with his friends called the Flying Machine in the 1960s and later went on to have a successful solo career. Taylor is known for his introspective lyrics, gentle voice, and acoustic guitar-based sound.

Match the genre with the period (5 years) most associated with it.- Singer Songwriter
Group of answer choices

60-65

65-70

70-75

75-80

The Singer-Songwriter genre is typically associated with the period 1970-1975. This was a period when artists such as Carole King, James Taylor, Cat Stevens, Joni Mitchell, and Neil Young gained widespread popularity for their introspective, personal songwriting and acoustic guitar-based sound.

Match the genre with the period (5 years) most associated with it.- Psychedelic
Group of answer choices

60-65

65-70

70-75

75-80

The Psychedelic genre is most associated with the period 1965-1970. This was a time when experimentation with drugs and mind-altering experiences influenced the music industry. Artists like The Beatles, The Doors, Jimi Hendrix, and Pink Floyd pushed traditional song structure boundaries and explored new sounds, mixing different genres and incorporating trippy lyrics and instrumentation.

Match the genre with the period (5 years) most associated with it.-Disco
Group of answer choices

60-65

70-75

65-70

75-80

The Disco genre is most associated with the period 1975-1980. The disco era emerged from the urban nightlife scenes in New York City in the mid-1970s. It quickly became popular, with its dance-oriented beats and infectious rhythm sections. Disco music featured prominently in dance clubs during this time with hits by artists like Bee Gees, Donna Summer, KC and The Sunshine Band, and Gloria Gaynor.

This 1970’s genre was influential on Punk and Rap.
Group of answer choices

Glam

Funk

Reggae

Corporate Rock

The genre that was influential on Punk and Rap in the 1970s is Funk. Funk music of the 1970s, pioneered by artists like James Brown, Sly and the Family Stone, and Parliament-Funkadelic, was influential on the development of both punk and rap. The heavy basslines and emphasis on rhythm and groove in funk were later incorporated into punk music, while the use of samples and repetitive beats in funk were key elements of early rap music.

Woodstock was the first big Rock festival ever.
Group of answer choices

True

False

False. While Woodstock is one of the most famous and iconic rock festivals of all time, it was not the first big rock festival. The Monterey Pop Festival, held in 1967 in California, is often regarded as the first major rock festival, attracting over 200,000 people and featuring performances by artists like The Who, Jimi Hendrix, and Janis Joplin.

According to the text, the anti-disco movement was  NOT a backlash against minorities and gays.
Group of answer choices

True

False

False. The anti-disco movement of the late 1970s was at least partially driven by a backlash against minority and gay cultures. Disco music emerged from urban, primarily African American, and LGBTQ+ communities in the major cities. Some segments of the population, particularly white, working-class men, saw disco as a threat to traditional rock and roll and viewed the dance-oriented, fashion-conscious culture surrounding disco as unmanly and un-American. Additionally, some opponents of disco music and culture held homophobic and racist beliefs and used the anti-disco movement as a means of expressing their prejudice.

Stevie Wonder never managed to break  away from his early Motown pop sound.
Group of answer choices

True

False

False. Stevie Wonder successfully broke away from his early Motown pop sound in the 1970s with a series of critically acclaimed and commercially successful albums that incorporated elements of funk, soul, jazz, and rock. Albums such as "Talking Book" (1972), "Innervisions" (1973), and "Songs in the Key of Life" (1976) are considered among his best work, and they explored complex musical and social issues with sophisticated, innovative arrangements and lyrics.

Drug use was a part of the psychedelic movement of the 1960’s.
Group of answer choices

True

False

True. Drug use was a significant part of the psychedelic movement of the 1960s. Many artists associated with the movement, such as The Beatles, Jimi Hendrix, and The Doors, experimented with LSD and other psychedelic drugs, which were believed to expand consciousness and induce mystical experiences. The use of drugs was closely tied to the music and culture of the time, and it played a major role in shaping the psychedelic aesthetic and ethos.

Eric Clapton formed The Yardbirds after leaving Cream.
Group of answer choices

True

False

False. Eric Clapton was a founding member of The Yardbirds and played with the band from 1963 to 1965. He left The Yardbirds due to creative differences and formed the band Cream in 1966 alongside Jack Bruce and Ginger Baker. Clapton's departure from Cream in 1968 allowed him to pursue a solo career and collaborate with other musicians, including George Harrison and Duane Allman.
did reggage influence disco music in the us

While there is no definitive evidence that reggae directly influenced disco music in the US, there are certainly shared elements between the two genres. Both disco and reggae have a strong emphasis on rhythm, groove, and danceability, and some disco songs feature reggae-style guitar riffs and syncopated beats. Additionally, both genres emerged as urban dance music scenes and share a similar social and cultural context. However, it is difficult to say whether reggae music directly influenced disco or whether they independently developed similar musical features in response to broader cultural trends and influences.

Dave Wakeling or Andy Cox was in which of these bands :Led Zeppelin

The Yardbirds

Cream

The Bryds

Neither Dave Wakeling nor Andy Cox was in Led Zeppelin, The Yardbirds, Cream, or The Byrds. They were both members of the 1980s British ska-pop band The English Beat (known in the US as The Beat) and later formed the band Fine Young Cannibals in the late 1980s.

A GUITARIST FROM GENERAL PUBLIC WAS IN ONE OF THESE BADS: Led Zeppelin

The Yardbirds

Cream

The Bryds

None of the members of General Public were in Led Zeppelin, The Yardbirds, Cream, or The Byrds. The members of General Public came from various other bands, such as The Beat, Dexys Midnight Runners, and The Clash.

Jefferson Airplane – White Rabbit: The artist modeled the flow of this song from a famous piece of classical music.

True

False

True. The flow of "White Rabbit" by Jefferson Airplane was modeled after a famous piece of classical music. The song's composer, Grace Slick, was inspired by Maurice Ravel's "Boléro," a Spanish-influenced classical music piece that features a repeating melody that builds in intensity over time. "White Rabbit" similarly has a repeating melody that gradually builds in intensity, featuring a memorable bassline and Grace Slick's distinctive vocals.
good vibrations the beach boys who was the producer

The producer of "Good Vibrations" by The Beach Boys was Brian Wilson. Brian Wilson was also the songwriter and arranger for the song, which features a complex arrangement of layered harmonies, unusual instrumentation, and innovative production techniques for the time. The song was recorded across multiple studios and sessions, and it is often regarded as one of the greatest and most innovative pop songs ever recorded.
who was the best selling black artist of the 1960s

The best selling black artist of the 1960s was Stevie Wonder. During that decade, Wonder had multiple hit records, including "Fingertips," "Uptight (Everything's Alright)," and "My Cherie Amour," among others. He was also a talented multi-instrumentalist, songwriter, and producer, and his music helped shaped the sound of Motown Records during the 1960s.

the members of the eagles were formerly  in the backing band for which artist?: 
Linda Ronstadt

Marie Osmond

Carly Simon

Carole King

The members of the Eagles were formerly in the backing band for Linda Ronstadt. Glenn Frey, Don Henley, Bernie Leadon, and Randy Meisner all played as session musicians in Ronstadt's backing band in the early 1970s. They later formed The Eagles in 1971, and their debut album was released in 1972.
who was the band lead in parliament

George Clinton was the band lead in Parliament. Clinton was a singer, songwriter, and record producer, and he is considered one of the key figures in the development of funk music. He founded the bands Parliament and Funkadelic in the 1970s, and his music was known for its wild, psychedelic sound, provocative lyrics, and theatrical stage shows. Clinton was known for his colorful costumes and wild hairstyle, and he has had a long and influential career in the music industry.

hippie psych funk needs to be one

My apologies for the error. The correct matching for Hippie Psychedelic Funk is Sly and the Family Stone. Sly and the Family Stone were known for blending psychedelic rock with funk and soul music in the late 1960s and early 1970s and are often associated with the counterculture and hippie movements.

which one is glam one needs glam

David Bowie represents Glam in this list. David Bowie was one of the most influential artists of the Glam Rock movement in the early 1970s. His flamboyant style, theatrical makeup, and multi-faceted musical approach made him an icon of the era. Other notable Glam Rock artists from the era included T. Rex, Gary Glitter, and Roxy Music.
who would be art rock

David Bowie is also associated with Art Rock, which combines elements of rock music with classical music, avant-garde techniques, and other forms of artistic expression. Other notable Art Rock bands include Pink Floyd, Genesis, and Yes. Art Rock is generally characterized by its use of complex instrumental arrangements, intricate song structures, and concept albums that often featured elaborate visual and thematic elements.
has to be one or the pther

My apologies for the mistake. To correct my previous answer, for Art Rock, it would be Pink Floyd. Pink Floyd is one of the most iconic and well-known Art Rock bands from the 1970s, famous for their complex, multi-layered soundscapes and their album-oriented approach to music. They were known for combining elements of rock, jazz, classical music, and experimental soundscapes to create a unique and innovative sound.

 where would glam go

Glam goes with David Bowie under Art Rock. Glam Rock was a sub-genre of Art Rock that emerged in the early 1970s and was characterized by its theatricality, androgynous fashion, and heavy reliance on visual spectacle. David Bowie was one of the key figures of the genre with his glam-inspired alter ego, Ziggy Stardust.

The Beatles' producer for most of their recordings was
Group of answer choices

Phil Spector

Gerry Coffey

Berry Gordy

George Martin

The Beatles' producer for most of their recordings was George Martin. He produced almost all of the Beatles' albums, starting with "Please Please Me" in 1963 and continuing until their breakup in 1970. Martin is often credited with helping to shape the Beatles' sound and encouraging their experimentation in the studio. He was known for his innovative production techniques and his ability to translate the band's creative ideas into recorded music.

The album that is consided the first concept album and spawned the idea of "art rock" is
Group of answer choices

Sgt. Peppers Lonely Hearts Club Band

Rubber Soul

Please Please Me

Let It Be

The album that is considered the first concept album and spawned the idea of "art rock" is "Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band" by The Beatles. Released in 1967, the album was a breakthrough in popular music and was widely regarded as a masterpiece. It featured a loose narrative framework of a fictional band, the titular Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band, and included experimental production techniques, avant-garde sound
